signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + BoJ Hawkishness
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.57 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite BoJ Deputy Governor Himino's warning of inflation overshoot risks and PM Takaichi's call for policy restraint. JPY COT LF net short -115,036 (29.2% long) remains extreme, while retail 84% short fades the institutional carry trade. 160.00 has been breached, but intervention risk remains acute as price holds above previous verbal warning levels. Carry dominates until VIX > 25.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -22,354 (23.9% long) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the July 8 meeting. Retail sentiment is 95% long, aligning with the hawkish OIS bias but directly contradicting crowded speculative shorts. This setup creates significant short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise. NZD/USD at 0.5731.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4181 — USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation at -0.513 (z=-1.95σ) marks a significant break from the -0.005 norm, as CAD fails to track energy moves. Retail 95% short vs COT net short -60,671 (24.7% long) shows retail and specs aligned in fading the USD/CAD rally. Implication: lack of institutional long conviction despite the 150bp carry advantage suggests a fragile trend.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Geopolitical Drag
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1463 — IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.8% (VRP +2.3%) signals elevated hedging demand as US-Iran peace uncertainty weighs on the pair. 25d RR at -0.67 confirms a mild put skew despite neutral CESI data. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25% (150bp spread) structurally favors USD, keeping upside limited toward 1.1500.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Political Uncertainty + Volatility
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3220 — Reports of PM Starmer's autumn departure introduce political risk premium. GBP/USD IV 11.0% vs HV30 6.7% (VRP +4.3%) reflects the highest vol premium among majors. While COT LF net long +21,961 supports the pound, the 25d RR at -0.82 indicates the options market is paying for downside protection against the spot trend.
